scope: Four key gaps: (1) William Hensley's parents; (2) Maxfield to Lee Stuart connection; (3) Lee Stuart Henslee's parents; (4) English/Welsh origins of the Hensley/Henslee surname.
sources_consulted: research/61-cousin-merged-people.json (GED data from Jenniffer Henslee cousin)
genealogytrails.com — Burleson County TX biographies (1910 publication)
granburydepot.org — Maxfield F. Henslee biography (Hood County, 1910)
ancestry.co.uk / geneanet — Hensley surname etymology (DAFN 2nd ed., 2022)
familysearch.org ancestor pages (Enoch Henslee 1832, James Hensley II)

gaps_addressed.title: William Hensley's parents (father of Macksfield Henslee)
gaps_addressed.finding: The cousin GED file (Jenniffer Henslee) contains an entry for William Hensley (birth 1693, Goochland VA — death 1770, Goochland VA) with wife Jane Snell and child Maxfield Henslee. WikiTree Hensley-196 places William Hensley (c.1691–c.1781) as father of Macksfield Hensley and names his parents as James Hensley II (1642–1705, Northampton VA) and Susanna (Newcomb) Hensley (1665–1730, Henrico VA). Multiple WikiTree profiles for other Hensley branches confirm James Hensley II and Susanna Newcomb as 2nd-great-grandparents of the Maxfield Henslee (1810) line, which traces back through William. HOWEVER, the Marty Grant researcher — the most rigorous Hensley genealogist online — explicitly states 'I have no clue who Mackfield's parents might be' and does NOT recognize the William Hensley (c.1691) → Macksfield chain. The WikiTree chain is asserted in user-contributed WikiTree profiles without cited primary source documentation. The Goochland County GED entry (1693 birth date) is from a secondary/user-submitted GED file and conflicts with the martygrant.com researcher's assessment. Treat as POSSIBLE pending primary source verification.
gaps_addressed.caveat: Martygrant.com (most authoritative independent researcher for this line) explicitly disclaims knowledge of Macksfield's parents. The WikiTree William Hensley (Hensley-196) chain to James Hensley II is user-asserted, not document-proven. The cousin GED file records William Hensley b.1693 Goochland + Jane Snell → Maxfield; this is the best available secondary pointer but not confirmed.

gaps_addressed.title: Maxfield G. Henslee (c.1810-1887) to Lee Stuart Henslee (1908) — intermediate generations
gaps_addressed.finding: The 1910 published biography of Lee W. Henslee (sheriff) establishes that Enoch Henslee (Gen 5, c.1832) and his wife Sarah (Hudson) Henslee had 10 children, of whom 4 survived to 1910: Laura, Nettie E. (Wood), E. Claude Henslee, and Lee W. Henslee (b.1873). The biography also confirms that Enoch moved to Texas in the 1850s, first settling in JACK COUNTY (not Burleson as previously recorded), then moving to Burleson County. Lee Stuart Henslee (b. 1908, d. 1994) was born in Texas, and Alice Marie Henslee was born in Rusk, Cherokee County TX in 1936 — suggesting Lee Stuart lived in Cherokee or adjacent East Texas counties. The intermediate generation between Enoch (Gen 5, b.1832) and Lee Stuart (b.1908) is ONE generation: Lee Stuart's father, probably born c.1875-1890. E. Claude Henslee (surviving sibling of Sheriff Lee W.) is a possible candidate for Lee Stuart's father, but no confirmation found. The 10-child household of Enoch also had 6 children who predeceased the 1910 publication — any of these could also have had children (including Lee Stuart's parent) before dying.
gaps_addressed.remaining_gap: Lee Stuart's father (one generation between Enoch Gen 5 and Lee Stuart) remains unidentified. Best research path: Texas Death Certificate for Lee Stuart Henslee (DSHS, $22 — names parents); 1900 and 1910 US Census for Henslee households in Cherokee/Rusk County TX (free on FamilySearch with account).

gaps_addressed.title: Lee Stuart Henslee's parents
gaps_addressed.finding: No free online source has been found naming Lee Stuart Henslee's parents. His birth date (1908-10-02) and burial (Beaumont TX, Forest Lawn) are confirmed. His daughter Alice Marie was born in Rusk TX (Cherokee County) in 1936, suggesting Lee Stuart was in East Texas at least through the mid-1930s. The Henslee cluster in Cherokee County is not extensively documented in free online sources. The most promising research approaches: Texas Death Certificate (Oct 1994, Jefferson County) which names informant and parents; 1920/1930 census records for Lee Henslee in East Texas; Texas Birth Index for 1908.
recommended_next_steps: Order Texas Death Certificate for Lee Stuart Henslee (died 1994-08-31, Jefferson County TX) from Texas DSHS — $22 — should name his parents
Search FamilySearch 1920 US Census for 'Lee Henslee' in Cherokee/Rusk/Jefferson County TX — free with browser account
Search FamilySearch 1930 US Census for 'Lee Henslee' household in East Texas — Alice would be ~7 years old showing parents

gaps_addressed.title: English/Welsh origins of the Hensley/Henslee surname
gaps_addressed.finding: Dictionary of American Family Names (Oxford, 2022 edition) confirms: Hensley/Henslee is an English HABITATIONAL surname from Hensley village in East Worlington parish, Devon, England. The name derives from Old English personal name Hēahmund (genitive Hēahmundes) + Old English lēah 'woodland clearing' — meaning 'Hēahmund's woodland clearing.' An alternative etymology from Hensleigh in Tiverton, Devon derives from Old English hengest (stallion) + leah. Both origin places are in Devon, England. The Henslee variant is specifically associated with the Macksfield line. The earliest English documentary record is 1297 (Yorkshire Subsidy Rolls: John de Henselay). Devon and Norfolk are the primary English counties of origin. Multiple early Virginia Hensley immigrants arrived 1635-1702, likely from Devon; the specific immigrant ancestor of Macksfield's line remains unverified.
gaps_addressed.confidence: CONFIRMED (Devon English origin of surname); POSSIBLE (specific immigrant ancestor)

conflicts_resolved.conflict: Enoch Henslee (Gen 5) settled in Jack County TX before Burleson County
conflicts_resolved.previous_record: research/65-henslee-confirmed-trace.json states Maxfield G. Henslee (Gen 4) 'came to Texas in 1851' and settled in Hunt/Collin/Hood Counties; Gen 5 Enoch shown in Burleson County
conflicts_resolved.resolution: 1910 biography of Lee W. Henslee clarifies that his father (Enoch, Gen 5) 'moved to Texas in the fifties, settling in Jack County, and from there moved to Burleson County.' Jack County is in north Texas — distinct from the Hood/Collin County trail of Gen 4's children by Phoebe Campbell. This confirms the Gen 5 Enoch (b.1832 GA) took a different migration path than the Hood County half-siblings (Maxfield F., Emma, Henry Wilson). Residence trail for Gen 5 Enoch should be updated to: Carroll Co GA → Jack County TX (c.1851-1858?) → Burleson County TX.
conflicts_resolved.source: Historical Review of South-East Texas Vol. 2 (Lewis Publishing, 1910) — Lee W. Henslee biography, via genealogytrails.com

conflicts_resolved.conflict: Enoch (Gen 5) had 10 children, not unknown number — only 4 survived to 1910
conflicts_resolved.previous_record: research/65-henslee-confirmed-trace.json lists Enoch (Gen 5) with wife 'Sarah J.' — maiden name noted as unknown
conflicts_resolved.resolution: 1910 biography confirms wife's maiden name was HUDSON — 'Enoch and Sarah (Hudson) Henslee, both natives of Georgia.' Both were Georgia-born, consistent with prior census data. The family had 10 children total; only Laura, Nettie E. (Wood), E. Claude, and Lee W. were still living by 1910. Six children predeceased. Lee Stuart Henslee (b.1908) was likely a grandchild of Enoch through one of these children.
conflicts_resolved.source: Historical Review of South-East Texas Vol. 2 (Lewis Publishing, 1910)

conflicts_unresolved.details: WikiTree Hensley-196 and cousin GED file both assert William Hensley (c.1691-1781) as Macksfield's father. Martygrant.com researcher (most thorough independent source for this line) explicitly states 'I have no clue who Mackfield's parents might be.' These are irreconcilable without a primary source. The WikiTree chain also asserts James Hensley II (1642) as William's father — also user-asserted only.
conflicts_unresolved.recommended_resolution: Search Hanover County VA deed and court records 1740-1760 for a Hensley/Henslee household with a son aged ~15-35. Check 1763 Hanover tax list context. Martygrant.com notes Macksfield 'first appears in 1763 as owner of 150 acres in what is now Hanover County.' A senior Hensley male in Hanover c.1740-1760 records would support the William hypothesis.

conflicts_unresolved.conflict: Enoch Henslee (Gen 5) death date and 'after 1880' vs. 'died 1916' per FamilySearch
conflicts_unresolved.details: Research file 65 states 'died after 1880' for Enoch (Gen 5, b.1832). The FamilySearch ancestor page for Enoch Henslee 1832-1916 suggests he may have lived to 1916. If so, he would still have been alive when Lee Stuart was born (1908) and Lee Stuart's father could be any of Enoch's children born before ~1888.
conflicts_unresolved.recommended_resolution: Verify Enoch's actual death date via Texas death records or Find a Grave. The 1916 death date (age ~84) is plausible given his son Lee W. was born 1873.

recommended_tree_additions.display_note: Display with a question mark on website tree; label as 'probable ancestor, unverified from primary source'
recommended_tree_additions.website_tag: Do NOT mark as immigrant — William appears American-born in Virginia Colony

recommended_tree_additions.update: Correct mother's maiden name from 'unknown' to 'Sarah Hudson' (confirmed from 1910 biography). Correct Texas migration path: Jack County TX first, then Burleson County (not direct to Burleson). Note that 10 children total, 4 survived to 1910.

recommended_tree_additions.subject: Lee Stuart Henslee parents
recommended_tree_additions.note: Parents still unidentified. Best candidates are children of Enoch (Gen 5) Henslee and Sarah Hudson, particularly E. Claude Henslee (surviving sibling of Sheriff Lee W., b.1873). Texas Death Certificate would resolve this definitively.

recommended_tree_additions.subject: Henslee English origin
recommended_tree_additions.note: Hensley/Henslee is a Devon, England habitational surname from Hensley village, East Worlington parish. Safe to note English Devon origin on website without asserting specific immigrant ancestor.

notable_new_findings.finding: Cousin GED file (61-cousin-merged-people.json) contains entry for William Hensley (b.1693 Goochland VA, d.1770 Goochland VA) + Jane Snell, with Maxfield Henslee as their child. This is internal to Hunter's own extended family tree data from a first cousin once removed (Jenniffer Henslee). This is the strongest secondary pointer to William as Macksfield's father, though still not a primary source.
notable_new_findings.significance: HIGH — family knowledge preserved in cousin's GED; warrants serious investigation

notable_new_findings.finding: WikiTree Hensley-196 names a chain: James Hensley II (1642, Northampton VA) + Susanna Newcomb (1665, Henrico VA) → William Hensley (c.1691, Fluvanna VA) + Jane Snell → Macksfield Hensley. If verified, this would extend Hunter's confirmed Henslee line back two additional generations to 1642 Virginia and locate the immigrant ancestor as James Hensley I (unknown, possibly late 1600s England immigrant to Virginia).
notable_new_findings.significance: HIGH — extends lineage by 2 generations if confirmed; positions James Hensley I as likely English immigrant c.1660-1685

notable_new_findings.finding: Sarah (Hudson) Henslee — wife of Enoch Henslee (Gen 5) — confirmed maiden name is HUDSON per 1910 Lewis Publishing biography. Previously listed as 'unknown' in research file 65.
notable_new_findings.significance: MEDIUM — corrects existing record; opens avenue for Hudson family research in Georgia

notable_new_findings.finding: Enoch Henslee (Gen 5) first settled in Jack County, TX (not Burleson) before moving to Burleson County. The 1910 biography states 'The father moved to Texas in the fifties, settling in Jack county, and from there moved to Burleson county.' Jack County was organized in 1857, suggesting arrival ~1857-1865.
notable_new_findings.significance: MEDIUM — corrects migration path in existing records

notable_new_findings.finding: Surname confirmation: Hensley/Henslee is definitively an English habitational name from Devon, England (Hensley village, East Worlington parish). Source: Oxford DAFN 2nd edition 2022. Derivation is from personal name Hēahmund + OE lēah (woodland clearing). The 'Henslee' variant is specific to the Macksfield line.
notable_new_findings.significance: LOW-MEDIUM — confirms English Devon origin for website display

Martygrant.com identifies at least 21 distinct William Hensleys in Virginia before 1800 — the specific William who fathered Macksfield is not identified by this researcher.

summary_for_website.what_is_now_known: The Henslee family traces to Macksfield Henslee (c.1725-1801) of Caswell County NC, a Revolutionary War payee (DAR/SAR qualifying). His father is most probably William Hensley (c.1691-c.1781) of Virginia — this is preserved in the family's own GED data from a first cousin — but remains unverified from primary sources and should not be displayed as confirmed on the tree. The surname is definitively English, from the village of Hensley in East Worlington parish, Devon, England, meaning 'Hēahmund's woodland clearing' in Old English.
summary_for_website.key_confirmed_update: Wife of Enoch Henslee (Gen 5, ancestor of Lee Stuart) is now confirmed as Sarah HUDSON (Georgia native) per 1910 published biography. Previously listed as maiden name unknown.
summary_for_website.migration_path_corrected: Enoch Henslee (Gen 5) settled in Jack County, Texas first (c.1857), then moved to Burleson County — not directly to Burleson as previously documented.
summary_for_website.still_missing: Lee Stuart Henslee's parents (the generation between Enoch's children and Lee Stuart) remain unidentified. A Texas Death Certificate ($22) would likely resolve this.
